content.text: Answer four problems. State clearly all the results from the text that you need, and write your answers clearly in complete English sentences. In all cases, be sure to justify your answer.

problem.text: Denote by \(Q_8\) the quaternion group. Show that if \(f:Q_8\to S_n\) is an injective homomorphism then \(n\geq 8\).
Show that any group of order \(80=2^4\cdot 5\) is solvable (do not simply quote Burnside’s theorem).

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: Define what it means for a group \(G\) to be nilpotent.

subparts.label: (ii)
subparts.text: Show that any nilpotent group is solvable.

subparts.label: (iii)
subparts.text: Give an example of a finite solvable group that is not nilpotent, and prove that this example is correct.

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: representatives of the isomorphism classes of abelian groups of order \(504=2^3\cdot 3^2\cdot 7\), and

subparts.label: (ii)
subparts.text: the number of elements of order \(6\) in each such group.

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: Find all conjugacy classes of elements of order \(2\) in \(S_6\), and the number of elements in each class.

problem.text:
Suppose \(p\) is prime, \(P\subseteq S_p\) is a Sylow \(p\)-subgroup of \(S_p\) and \(N=N(P)\) is the normalizer of \(P\). Show that
\[
N\simeq (\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z})\rtimes_\varphi(\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z})^\times
\]
and describe \(\varphi\).
